author: w3Lb
content: RESOLVED BY MEASUREMENT, not implementation. The premise (CPU-bound replacement in large files) was eliminated by #108+#109 before this issue was reached: on the 64MiB corpus, literal replace is now 0.14s user / 1.5s wall (I/O-bound, already faster than sed), plain regex 0.26s user, capture-heavy regex 0.45s user of 1.55s wall (sigma 0.45s — perfect parallelization of user time would land inside run noise), --transform 0.21s user. Line-boundary chunking would add real engine complexity (chunk/context-cap/budget coordination, rayon dep in the pure-logic crate) for a gain smaller than measurement noise on every probed workload. Trigger to REOPEN: any future per-line workload measured at >1s user time on large files (e.g. heavy backtracking regex via a future fancy-regex feature, or per-line ops gaining expensive logic). Inter-file parallelism (#97) remains the parallelism story and is unaffected.
